wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the OpenYida project overview section focused on introducing this AI-powered CLI tool for Alibaba Yida low-code platform development. Explain its purpose as a zero-configuration, instant-deploy solution that bridges natural language AI commands with enterprise-grade low-code application creation. Document the core value proposition of enabling developers to build complex business systems through conversational AI without traditional coding barriers. Include the project's architecture overview, target audience (developer-focused), supported AI coding tools integration, and relationship to Alibaba Yida platform. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like IPD systems, CRM applications, and utility tools. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'Yida app', 'low-code capabilities', and 'AI skill integration'. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners understanding low-code development and technical details for experienced developers evaluating AI-assisted development tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive application lifecycle documentation for OpenYida's application management capabilities. Document the complete application creation process from natural language requirements to deployed Yida applications, including AI-assisted schema generation and automated code creation. Explain application export/import functionality for migration and backup purposes. Detail application configuration options, branding settings, and corporate customization features. Document the relationship between applications, forms, pages, and data models in the Yida ecosystem. Include practical examples of building complex business systems like IPD workflows, CRM applications, and utility tools. Address troubleshooting common development issues, schema validation errors, and deployment failures. Explain application versioning, migration strategies, and best practices for application architecture and naming conventions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Login Flows & Authentication Methods
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for OpenYida's login flow implementations. Document the dual authentication mechanisms including QR code-based login and DingTalk auto-login detection. Explain the browser automation process using Playwright for seamless authentication experiences. Detail the login flow orchestration, including automatic login detection, QR code generation and scanning, and session establishment. Document the integration with Alibaba Yida platform authentication systems, CSRF token extraction, and cookie management. Include step-by-step authentication workflows, error handling for failed login attempts, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of login scenarios for different user types and organizational setups. Address troubleshooting common login issues including network connectivity problems, authentication timeouts, and platform-specific authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment & Authentication Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's environment and authentication command group. Document the env command for detecting AI tool environments and login status, login command variants including QR code authentication (--qr), logout functionality, and organization management commands (list, switch). For each command, provide syntax, parameters, options, usage examples, and expected outputs. Explain the authentication flow including session persistence, cookie management, and organization switching mechanisms. Include troubleshooting guidance for common authentication issues, network connectivity problems, and session timeout scenarios. Document command aliases, default behaviors, and integration with the broader CLI ecosystem. Address security considerations for credential storage and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unified Data Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's unified data operations system. Document the core data management API covering forms, processes, tasks, and subforms with consistent CRUD operations. Explain the command structure including query, get, create, update operations for each resource type. Detail parameter validation, session management, and authentication requirements. Document the CLI interface with usage patterns, parameter options, and error handling. Include practical examples of form data operations, process instance management, and task execution workflows. Explain the relationship between different data entities and their interdependencies. Address performance considerations for bulk operations and large dataset handling. Provide troubleshooting guides for common data operation errors and authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Creation & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for report creation and management workflows in OpenYida. Document the complete report creation process from initial setup through publishing, including report schema generation, configuration options, and validation steps. Explain the report lifecycle management including creation, modification, deletion, and version control. Detail the integration with Alibaba Yida platform APIs for report registration and metadata management. Include practical examples of creating different types of reports such as sales reports, operational dashboards, and business intelligence summaries. Document report naming conventions, categorization systems, and organizational structure. Explain report dependencies, data source connections, and metadata management. Address common issues during report creation including schema validation errors, API connectivity problems, and permission-related failures. Provide troubleshooting guides for report deployment issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connector Management & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for connector management and configuration in OpenYida's HTTP connector system. Document the complete workflow for creating, updating, listing, and managing HTTP connectors. Explain connector creation parameters including name, base URL, authentication types, and icon configuration. Detail the connector update process for modifying descriptions and properties without changing the underlying API structure. Cover connector listing functionality with filtering, pagination, and display options. Document connector detail retrieval including security schemes, operations, and metadata. Explain connector testing procedures for validating API connectivity and operation execution. Include authentication configuration options such as basic auth, API key authentication, DingTalk OAuth, Aliyun API Gateway, and DingTrust gateway. Provide practical examples of connector setup for different authentication scenarios. Address common configuration errors, troubleshooting steps, and best practices for connector management.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Diagnostics & Environment Checks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's system diagnostics and environment checking capabilities. Document the DiagnosticEngine architecture with its three-layer checker system (EnvironmentChecker, ApplicationChecker, FixEngine). Explain environment validation including Node.js version checks, Python requirements, Playwright installation verification, gh CLI setup, Skills configuration, login status validation, and network connectivity testing. Detail application-level diagnostics covering PRD file validation, page source code analysis, schema cache integrity checks, and React Hooks usage pattern detection. Include practical examples of diagnostic output interpretation, common environment issues and their fixes, automated repair processes, and manual intervention requirements. Document the severity levels (ERROR, WARNING, INFO), fix types (AUTO, MANUAL, COMMAND), and troubleshooting workflows for each diagnostic category.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill Architecture & Core Concepts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's AI skill architecture and core concepts. Explain the skill system design pattern, plugin architecture, and template-based code generation framework. Document the skill metadata structure, compatibility matrix, and workflow orchestration. Detail the relationship between main skill and sub-skills, including skill dependency chains and execution order. Explain the skill discovery mechanism, loading process, and runtime environment management. Include practical examples of skill configuration, parameter passing, and result handling. Document the skill lifecycle from registration to execution, including error handling and fallback mechanisms. Address skill versioning, compatibility checking, and upgrade procedures. Provide guidelines for skill development, testing, and debugging within the OpenYida ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internal APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive internal API documentation for OpenYida's core utility functions and system interfaces. Document the utility functions including project root detection, cookie parsing, authentication helpers, and HTTP request utilities. Detail the internationalization system with translation functions and locale management. Explain environment detection mechanisms and diagnostic tools. Include function signatures, parameter specifications, return values, and error handling patterns. Provide practical examples of API usage in different contexts such as authentication flows, data operations, and system diagnostics. Document the internal API contracts, data structures, and integration patterns used throughout the OpenYida ecosystem. Address performance considerations, security implications, and best practices for internal API consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: QR Code Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's QR code authentication implementation. Detail the complete QR code generation process, including QR code creation, display, and scanning workflow. Explain the browser automation using Playwright for seamless user experience during QR code authentication. Document the cookie extraction process from the authenticated session, CSRF token validation, and base URL resolution for both standard and custom domain configurations. Include step-by-step authentication flow from QR code generation to session establishment. Address timeout handling, retry mechanisms, and error recovery strategies. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common issues such as QR code scanning failures, network connectivity problems, and browser compatibility issues. Document the integration with Alibaba Yida platform authentication endpoints and cookie management for session persistence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structure Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's structure validation system. Detail the mandatory directory and file requirements including bin/, lib/, project/, yida-skills/, scripts/ directories and essential files like package.json, README.md, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, and .eslintrc.json. Explain the validation logic that checks for required project structure, verifies Node.js engine requirements through package.json engines field, counts JavaScript modules in the lib/ directory, and enumerates AI skill packages. Include practical examples of running the validation script, interpreting validation errors, fixing missing directories and files, configuring Node.js engine versions, and organizing project structure according to validation standards. Document the integration with development workflows and CI/CD pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Checks
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's environment validation system. Document the EnvironmentChecker class architecture and its ten validation categories: Node.js version compatibility (v16+ requirement), Python installation and version checks (3.10+ minimum), Playwright framework verification including Chromium browser installation, GitHub CLI setup and authentication status, project configuration file validation (config.json syntax and existence), AI Skills package detection and installation status, Alibaba Yida platform login state verification with cookie validation, and network connectivity testing to aliwork.com. Explain the severity levels (ERROR, WARNING, INFO), fix types (AUTO, MANUAL, COMMAND), and provide practical examples of each check result. Include troubleshooting steps for common environment issues, dependency installation commands, and manual intervention requirements. Document the automated repair capabilities for config.json creation and invalid schema cache cleanup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Update Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's update management system. Document the automatic version checking mechanism that queries the npm registry for the latest version of the openyida package. Explain the semantic version comparison logic used to determine if updates are available, including major, minor, and patch version detection. Detail the asynchronous update checking process that doesn't block main command execution and handles network timeouts gracefully. Include practical examples of how update notifications are displayed, configuration options for controlling update checks, and troubleshooting steps for network connectivity issues. Document the relationship between update checks and system health monitoring, including how failed update checks don't impact core functionality. Address customization of update frequency, notification preferences, and integration with CI/CD pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Creation Skill
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the yida-create-app skill package. Document the application creation workflow through the registerApp API, including parameter specifications (appName, description, icon, iconColor), authentication requirements with cookies.json, and integration with the broader OpenYida development workflow. Explain the skill's role as the first step in application development, its relationship with subsequent skills like yida-create-form-page and yida-create-page, and the output format containing appType and admin URL. Include practical examples of basic and advanced usage, error handling mechanisms, and the icon/color selection system. Address the skill's compatibility with AI coding platforms and its position in the skill chain workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for OpenYida focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Document installation procedures using npm with global installation, system requirements (Node.js ≥ 18), and environment verification steps. Explain the zero-configuration approach and how OpenYida works out of the box with AI coding tools. Include step-by-step setup instructions for supported AI platforms (Claude Code, Aone Copilot, OpenCode, Cursor, VS Code, Qoder, Wukong). Provide initial authentication flow using QR code login and session management. Document the basic workflow from natural language requirements to deployed Yida applications. Include common first commands like 'openyida env', 'openyida login', and simple application creation examples. Address troubleshooting for common setup issues and environment detection problems. Make content accessible to developers new to AI-assisted development while providing clear next steps for advanced usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Management & Schema Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed form management documentation for OpenYida's form creation and configuration capabilities. Document the complete form schema creation process including field types, validation rules, and form layouts. Explain form field types such as text inputs, dropdowns, date pickers, file uploads, and custom components. Detail form validation patterns, conditional logic, and dynamic field behavior. Document form configuration options including visibility rules, read-only states, and default values. Explain the relationship between forms and underlying data models, including field mapping and data persistence. Include practical examples of building complex forms for HR workflows, expense approvals, and customer management. Address form permission management, access control, and role-based field visibility. Document troubleshooting common form issues, validation errors, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management & Token Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive session management documentation for OpenYida's authentication system. Detail the session persistence mechanism using local cache files (.cache/auth.json), token extraction and validation processes, and automatic session refresh capabilities. Explain CSRF token management, cookie data handling, and base URL resolution for different environments. Document the session lifecycle from initial login through automatic refresh to logout cleanup. Include token storage security considerations, cache invalidation strategies, and session state validation. Provide examples of session status checking, token renewal processes, and handling expired or invalid sessions. Address session persistence across different operating systems and development environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application & Form Management Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for OpenYida's application and form management command group. Document create-app command for application creation with name, description, icon, and theme parameters. Cover create-form subcommands including create and update operations with field JSON specification, layout options, and theme customization. Document get-schema for retrieving form schemas, update-form-config for modifying form settings like navigation rendering and titles. For each command, provide comprehensive syntax, parameter descriptions, JSON schema examples, and practical usage scenarios. Include form field type documentation, validation rule configuration, and layout customization options. Address common form development patterns, schema modification workflows, and integration with the broader application lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Query Systems & Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenYida's query systems and search capabilities. Document advanced filtering mechanisms including date ranges, status filters, and custom search conditions. Explain pagination controls, sorting options, and result ordering. Detail the search JSON format and parameter mapping for complex queries. Document subform querying with table field filtering and hierarchical data retrieval. Explain task query types (todo, done, submitted, cc) and their specific parameters. Include examples of complex search scenarios, performance optimization techniques, and query result processing. Address query result formatting, data transformation, and export capabilities. Provide guidance on query debugging, performance tuning, and best practices for efficient data retrieval.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chart Types & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for chart types and configuration options in OpenYida's reporting system. Document all available chart types including line trend charts, multi-bar comparison charts, radar charts, scatter plots, stacked areas, and china map visualizations. For each chart type, explain configuration parameters, data binding requirements, styling options, and customization capabilities. Detail the chart schema structure, property definitions, and validation rules. Include practical examples showing how to configure each chart type with real-world data scenarios. Document advanced features like drill-down capabilities, interactive filters, real-time data updates, and responsive design considerations. Explain chart performance optimization techniques for large datasets, memory management, and rendering efficiency. Address common chart configuration issues including data formatting problems, styling conflicts, and rendering errors. Provide best practices for chart selection based on data types and business requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Smart Connector Creation & API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's smart connector creation system and API integration capabilities. Document the automatic connector generation process using curl commands and API documentation parsing. Explain how the system analyzes existing API endpoints to create connector configurations automatically. Detail the API parsing algorithms that extract endpoint definitions, request/response schemas, and parameter specifications. Cover template generation for different API patterns and integration styles. Document the integration with popular API documentation formats like OpenAPI/Swagger specifications. Explain how the system maps API operations to connector actions and generates appropriate authentication configurations. Include advanced features like batch operation creation, parameter validation, and response transformation rules. Provide step-by-step examples of converting existing curl commands into working connectors. Address limitations, supported API formats, and troubleshooting common parsing issues. Document best practices for optimizing generated connectors and manual customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Framework & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenYida's validation framework and quality assurance scripts. Document the structure validation system that ensures proper project organization, file naming conventions, and directory layouts. Explain dependency validation processes that check package.json integrity, version compatibility, and missing dependencies. Detail internationalization validation covering locale file completeness, translation coverage, and localization consistency checks. Include practical examples of running validation scripts, interpreting validation results, fixing common structural issues, resolving dependency conflicts, and maintaining translation accuracy. Document the integration of validation scripts into development workflows, CI/CD pipeline integration, and automated quality gates. Address validation rule customization, exception handling, and performance optimization for large projects.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Tool Integration & Compatibility
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AI tool integration and compatibility within OpenYida's skill system. Document support for major AI coding platforms including Claude Code, Aone Copilot, OpenCode, Cursor, VS Code, Qoder, and Wukong. Explain the integration mechanisms, authentication flows, and capability detection for each AI tool. Detail the compatibility matrix showing which features work with each platform and any limitations or workarounds. Document the environment detection process, tool switching mechanisms, and session management across different AI platforms. Include troubleshooting guides for common integration issues, performance optimization tips, and best practices for each supported AI tool. Provide configuration examples and setup procedures for optimal skill execution across different AI coding environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: External API Integrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed external API integration documentation for OpenYida's connector system and Yida platform API interactions. Document the HTTP connector creation process, API endpoint specifications, and authentication mechanisms. Explain the connector lifecycle from creation to testing and deployment. Detail the request/response patterns, data transformation processes, and error handling strategies. Include connector configuration options, action definitions, and parameter mapping. Document the integration with Alibaba Yida platform APIs, including endpoint URLs, authentication protocols, and rate limiting considerations. Provide practical examples of connector implementations for common use cases such as CRM integration, HR systems, and external data sources. Address security considerations, SSL/TLS requirements, and proxy configurations for enterprise environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: DingTalk Auto-Login Detection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenYida's DingTalk auto-login detection mechanism. Explain the automatic login detection algorithm that identifies existing DingTalk login sessions without requiring manual intervention. Document the session validation process, including cookie parsing, CSRF token verification, and organization context detection. Detail the fallback logic that triggers QR code authentication when auto-login detection fails. Include examples of different login scenarios including fresh installations, cached sessions, expired sessions, and multi-organization environments. Address the integration with DingTalk platform authentication, session persistence mechanisms, and automatic renewal processes. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common auto-login issues such as session expiration, browser compatibility, and platform-specific authentication challenges. Document the configuration options for controlling auto-login behavior and security considerations for session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dependency Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenYida's dependency validation system. Explain the dependency checking mechanisms that verify package.json integrity, validate version compatibility across dependencies, detect missing or conflicting packages, and ensure proper dependency resolution. Document the validation criteria for production dependencies, development dependencies, peer dependencies, and optional dependencies. Include practical examples of dependency validation failures, common dependency conflicts and their resolutions, updating dependency versions, managing peer dependency requirements, and optimizing dependency trees. Address integration with npm/yarn workflows, automated dependency updates, and dependency security scanning integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for OpenYida's application-level diagnostic system. Document the ApplicationChecker class functionality covering four validation areas: PRD documentation assessment including directory existence and markdown file validation, page source code analysis with empty file detection and console.log statement scanning, schema cache integrity verification with JSON parsing validation and corrupted file cleanup, and React Hooks usage pattern detection including conditional rendering violations and proper hook placement rules. Explain the automated detection algorithms, common code patterns that trigger warnings, and remediation strategies. Include practical examples of problematic code patterns, their detection logic, and suggested fixes. Document the code block parsing mechanism for hook validation and provide guidelines for maintaining React best practices in low-code applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Installation & Setup Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for OpenYida's installation and setup automation system. Document the post-installation script that handles initial setup tasks including dependency validation, locale synchronization, and environment configuration. Explain the project structure validation system that ensures proper directory layout and file organization. Detail the internationalization synchronization process that maintains consistency across multiple language files. Include practical examples of running installation automation, customizing setup parameters, and troubleshooting installation failures. Document the relationship between installation automation and system initialization, including how automated tasks contribute to overall system reliability. Address customization options for installation workflows, validation rules, and locale management processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Page Creation Skill
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the yida-create-form-page skill package. Document the form page creation process, including form schema generation, field type definitions, validation rules, and page configuration options. Explain the skill's integration with the application creation workflow, parameter requirements for form definition, and the relationship with form permission management. Include examples of form creation commands, schema structure requirements, and the connection between form pages and data management operations. Address form customization options, field validation patterns, and the skill's role in the overall low-code development pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: CLI Command Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CLI command reference documentation for OpenYida's command-line interface. Document all 50+ available commands organized by functional categories: Environment & Authentication, Application & Form Management, Page Configuration & Sharing, Data Management, Permissions & Process, HTTP Connector System, Report & Dashboard, and CDN Integration. For each command, provide syntax, parameters, options, usage examples, and expected outputs. Include command aliases, default behaviors, and common parameter combinations. Document the command routing mechanism and how new commands are integrated into the system. Explain command chaining patterns and workflow automation possibilities. Address error handling, return codes, and troubleshooting common command failures. Include practical examples showing typical development workflows from requirement gathering to deployment. Provide both basic usage patterns for beginners and advanced parameter combinations for experienced users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Page Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed custom page development documentation for OpenYida's React-like JSX page creation system. Document the page development workflow from initial template creation to deployment and sharing. Explain the JSX syntax extensions used for Yida pages, component composition patterns, and state management approaches. Detail page configuration options including layout settings, responsive design, and mobile optimization. Document the page publishing process, including build compilation, asset optimization, and deployment to Yida platform. Explain page sharing and access control mechanisms, including public URLs and restricted access modes. Include practical examples of building interactive dashboards, data visualization pages, and utility tools. Address page performance optimization, caching strategies, and troubleshooting common development issues. Document integration patterns with forms, reports, and external APIs.

Address security considerations for cross-tenant data isolation and access control enforcement.

For each command, provide syntax, parameters, file path requirements, and deployment workflows. Document sharing configuration options including public access controls, open authentication settings, and URL verification mechanisms. Include practical examples of page development workflows, sharing strategies, and access control implementations. Address common deployment issues, URL shortening services, and troubleshooting sharing configuration problems.

Address enterprise security requirements and compliance considerations for authentication processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Application Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document advanced application development features in OpenYida including process workflows, report integration, and CDN management. Explain process workflow configuration for business process automation, including approval chains, validation rules, and notification systems. Detail report creation and chart integration for data visualization, including dashboard components and real-time data binding. Document CDN configuration and image management for enterprise applications, including upload workflows and cache optimization. Explain the integration patterns between forms, processes, reports, and pages within complex business applications. Include practical examples of building comprehensive business systems with integrated workflows, analytics, and user interfaces. Address advanced configuration options, performance tuning, and troubleshooting complex integration scenarios. Document best practices for scalable application architecture and maintainable code organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Logout Procedures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security documentation for OpenYida's authentication system security measures and logout procedures. Document secure token storage practices, credential protection mechanisms, and session termination processes. Explain the logout workflow including local cache cleanup, token invalidation, and browser session termination. Detail security considerations for authentication data storage, encryption of sensitive information, and protection against session hijacking attacks. Include best practices for enterprise security compliance, audit logging, and monitoring authentication activities. Address security troubleshooting including suspicious login detection, unauthorized access prevention, and incident response procedures. Document integration with enterprise security policies and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management & Permission Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for OpenYida's data management and permission command group. Document the data command for unified data operations across forms, processes, tasks, and sub-forms, including query, filter, and update operations. Cover permission management commands including get-permission for retrieving permission configurations and save-permission for setting data and action permissions. Document process workflow commands configure-process and create-process for workflow definition and configuration. For each command, provide comprehensive syntax, parameter options, data filtering capabilities, and permission schema examples. Include practical examples of data querying patterns, permission configuration workflows, and process automation setups. Address data validation, access control patterns, and workflow rule configuration. Document integration with form schemas, process definitions, and permission hierarchies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extensibility & Custom Skill Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for extending OpenYida's AI skill system and developing custom skills. Explain the extensibility model, plugin architecture, and development guidelines for creating new AI skill integrations. Document the skill development lifecycle from concept to deployment, including testing procedures and validation requirements. Detail the API specifications, interface contracts, and integration patterns for custom skills. Include step-by-step tutorials for creating new skill packages, implementing AI tool adapters, and integrating third-party services. Document the skill packaging process, distribution mechanisms, and community contribution guidelines. Address advanced topics like skill chaining, conditional logic, performance optimization, and error handling strategies. Provide templates, boilerplate code, and best practices for successful skill development within the OpenYida ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Skills
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for authentication-related skill packages including yida-login and yida-logout. Document the login workflow with QR code authentication, session management through cookies.json, CSRF token handling, and logout procedures. Explain the authentication skill's role as a foundational component for all other skills, parameter requirements for login commands, and session persistence mechanisms. Include examples of login/logout operations, error handling for authentication failures, and the skill's integration with the Playwright browser automation system. Address security considerations, session timeout handling, and troubleshooting authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive application development documentation for OpenYida's low-code application creation capabilities. Document the complete application lifecycle from natural language requirements to deployed Yida applications. Explain form schema creation, field types, validation rules, and form configuration options. Detail custom page development using React-like JSX syntax, page publishing workflows, and deployment processes. Document the AI-assisted development process including natural language to schema translation and automated code generation. Include advanced features like form permissions, process integration, and report embedding. Explain the relationship between forms, pages, and applications in the Yida ecosystem. Provide practical examples of building complex business systems like IPD workflows, CRM applications, and utility tools. Address troubleshooting common development issues, schema validation errors, and deployment failures. Include best practices for application architecture, naming conventions, and development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP Connector & Report Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OpenYida's HTTP connector and report command group. Document the connector command group including list, create, detail, delete, add-action, list-actions, delete-action, test, list-connections, create-connection, smart-create, parse-api, and gen-template subcommands. Cover report commands create-report and append for chart-based reporting with multiple visualization types. For each command, provide syntax, parameter options, API integration patterns, and report configuration examples. Explain HTTP connector setup for external API integration, action management, authentication configuration, and testing procedures. Document report chart types, data binding patterns, dashboard component integration, and visualization customization. Include practical examples of connector development workflows, API integration patterns, and report creation processes. Address common integration challenges, authentication troubleshooting, and report performance optimization.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# OpenYIDA Project Overview

## Project Introduction

OpenYIDA is a comprehensive CLI tool designed for Alibaba Yida (DingTalk Low-Code Platform) development, providing AI-powered application creation capabilities. The project enables developers to create, manage, and deploy low-code applications through natural language commands and automated workflows.

### Purpose Statement
OpenYIDA serves as a unified development environment that bridges AI coding tools with Alibaba Yida's low-code platform, eliminating the complexity of manual application development and deployment processes.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Zero-configuration Development**: Install once, develop anywhere with automatic environment detection
- **AI-Powered Application Creation**: Natural language to application generation workflow
- **Multi-Environment Support**: Seamless integration with various AI coding platforms (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, etc.)
- **Complete Development Lifecycle**: From concept to deployment with automated tooling

### Target Audience
- Enterprise developers working with Alibaba Yida platform
- Teams seeking rapid application development without deep frontend expertise
- Organizations adopting low-code development strategies

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular CLI architecture with clear separation of concerns across different functional domains.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[CLI Entry Point] --> B[Command Router]
    B --> C[Authentication Module]
    B --> D[Application Management]
    B --> E[Page Development]
    B --> F[Data Management]
    B --> G[Integration Services]
    
    C --> H[Login Management]
    C --> I[Session Handling]
    
    D --> J[App Creation]
    D --> K[Schema Management]
    D --> L[Publish Pipeline]
    
    E --> M[Custom Page Engine]
    E --> N[Build System]
    E --> O[Deployment]
    
    F --> P[Form Data Operations]
    F --> Q[Process Management]
    F --> R[Report Generation]
    
    G --> S[Connector Management]
    G --> T[API Integration]
    G --> U[External Services]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **bin/**: Command-line interface entry points
- **lib/**: Core library modules organized by functionality
- **project/**: Template workspace structure for development
- **yida-skills/**: AI skill packages for different coding environments
- **scripts/**: Build and maintenance automation scripts

**Design Patterns:**
- **Command Pattern**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate module
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic module loading based on command execution
- **Strategy Pattern**: Multiple AI coding platform integrations
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for login state and project changes

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI
    participant Auth
    participant YidaAPI
    participant Browser
    
    User->>+CLI: openyida create-app "My App"
    CLI->>+Auth: ensureLogin()
    Auth->>+Browser: Launch login browser
    Browser-->>-Auth: Authentication complete
    Auth-->>-CLI: Valid session
    CLI->>+YidaAPI: Create application
    YidaAPI-->>-CLI: App created successfully
    CLI-->>-User: Display app URL
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **bin/yida.js**: Primary CLI entry point handling all command routing
- **lib/core/utils.js**: Shared utilities for HTTP requests, authentication, and project management
- **lib/core/i18n.js**: Internationalization support for multiple languages

### Core Modules
- **lib/auth/**: Complete authentication system with Playwright-based browser automation
- **lib/app/**: Application lifecycle management (create, publish, manage)
- **lib/core/**: Foundation utilities and common functionality
- **lib/connector/**: HTTP connector management for external API integration
- **lib/report/**: Report creation and chart visualization capabilities

### Configuration Approach
- **project/config.json**: Environment-specific configuration settings
- **package.json**: Dependencies, scripts, and metadata
- **yida-skills/**: Skill packages for different AI coding environments

### External Dependencies
- **@babel/standalone**: JavaScript transpilation for custom page compilation
- **playwright**: Browser automation for login and testing
- **qrcode**: QR code generation for authentication flows
- **uglify-js**: JavaScript minification for production builds

### Integration Points
- **Alibaba Yida Platform**: Direct API integration for application management
- **AI Coding Platforms**: Integration with Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, and others
- **External APIs**: HTTP connector system for third-party service integration

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[CLI Commands] --> B[Authentication Layer]
    A --> C[Application Layer]
    A --> D[Development Tools]
    
    B --> E[Session Cache]
    B --> F[Browser Automation]
    
    C --> G[App Registry]
    C --> H[Schema Management]
    C --> I[Publish Pipeline]
    
    D --> J[Build System]
    D --> K[Template Engine]
    D --> L[Deployment Manager]
    
    E --> M[Yida API]
    G --> M
    H --> M
    I --> M
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Application Development Workflow:**
- Natural language application creation
- Automated project scaffolding
- Multi-environment deployment
- Real-time collaboration features

**Development Tools:**
- Custom page development with React-like JSX
- Form builder with drag-and-drop interface
- Report generation with chart integration
- Process workflow configuration

**Integration Capabilities:**
- HTTP connector management
- External API integration
- Third-party service connectivity
- Data synchronization across platforms

### Implementation Highlights

**Smart Authentication System:**
- Automatic detection of AI coding environments
- Persistent session management
- Cross-platform compatibility
- QR code-based login flow

**AI-Powered Development:**
- Skill-based integration with multiple AI platforms
- Context-aware code generation
- Template-based application creation
- Intelligent suggestion systems

**Development Environment:**
- Zero-configuration setup
- Automatic dependency management
- Cross-platform compatibility
- Extensible plugin architecture

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> EnvironmentDetection
    EnvironmentDetection --> LoginCheck: Auto-login available
    EnvironmentDetection --> BrowserLogin: Manual login required
    
    LoginCheck --> ProjectInit: Valid session
    BrowserLogin --> ProjectInit: Login successful
    
    ProjectInit --> AppCreation: New application
    ProjectInit --> PageDevelopment: Existing project
    
    AppCreation --> SchemaGeneration: App registered
    SchemaGeneration --> PageDevelopment: Schema ready
    
    PageDevelopment --> BuildProcess: Ready to compile
    BuildProcess --> Deployment: Build successful
    
    Deployment --> [*]: Application live
    
    PageDevelopment --> DataManagement: Need data operations
    DataManagement --> PageDevelopment: Data operations complete
```

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [bin/yida.js](bin/yida.js)
- [lib/core/i18n.js](lib/core/i18n.js)
- [lib/app/create-app.js](lib/app/create-app.js)
- [lib/core/utils.js](lib/core/utils.js)
- [project/config.json](project/config.json)
- [yida-skills/SKILL.md](yida-skills/SKILL.md)
- [project/pages/src/demo-birthday-game.js](project/pages/src/demo-birthday-game.js)
- [yida-skills/skills/yida-custom-page/SKILL.md](yida-skills/skills/yida-custom-page/SKILL.md)
- [scripts/postinstall.js](scripts/postinstall.js)
- [lib/auth/login.js](lib/auth/login.js)
- [lib/core/check-update.js](lib/core/check-update.js)

</blog>
